title: Valoon Marketplace listing manifest
description: Single JSON document that fully describes an app or integration for the Valoon Marketplace. Partners submit this file; once approved it becomes the catalog entry and drives the connect flow.

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this schema, for editor validation.

manifest_version.description: Version of the manifest format. Currently always 1.

slug.description: Unique kebab-case identifier. It is the marketplace URL segment (/listing/<slug>) and the key used to upsert the catalog entry.

type.description: app = runs on the user's infrastructure (download/installer/MCP). integration = runs as an external service that connects to the user's Valoon account.

category.description: Kebab-case category slug used by catalog filters (e.g. communication, sync, time-tracking, ai, export).

availability.description: coming_soon lists the entry in the catalog without an active install/connect flow.

made_by.description: Who builds and operates it. Partner submissions are always registered as partner regardless of this value.

certified.description: Valoon certification badge. Set by Valoon during review — values in partner submissions are ignored.

icon.description: HTTPS URL of a square icon (SVG or PNG, ≥128px). When omitted the marketplace renders an initials placeholder.

works_with.description: Valoon entities the listing reads or writes: projects, tickets, reports, users, time-entries, media, files, labels, …

platforms.description: Apps only: platforms it runs on (e.g. Windows, macOS, Linux, Web).

external_service.description: Integrations only: the external service it bridges to Valoon (e.g. WhatsApp, Google Drive, SAP).

stack.description: Short human-readable tech stack line shown on the detail page (e.g. "Node.js · Express · PostgreSQL").

links.description: External links rendered on the detail page. All optional.

mcp.description: Remote MCP server endpoint. When present the install flow renders client config snippets for it.

i18n.description: Catalog copy per locale. English is required; de/es/fr are strongly recommended (the marketplace falls back to en, then de).

connection.description: How the marketplace hands a user over to the listing.

method.description: handoff = full marketplace→integration token handoff (see docs/protocol.md). api_key = the marketplace only walks the user through creating/pasting a Valoon API key (apps that hold the key themselves, e.g. MCP clients). none = nothing to connect (plain downloads).

redirect_url.description: handoff only: HTTPS endpoint of YOUR backend that receives the user after the marketplace flow. The marketplace appends ?handoff_token=vmh_… — your backend claims it server-side. Must be HTTPS in production.

config_fields.description: Setup inputs the marketplace collects in its connect flow and delivers inside the handoff payload (config object). Keep it to what you need before first contact — everything else belongs in your own UI after the handoff.

contact.description: Who Valoon contacts about this listing. Not shown publicly.

sort_order.description: Catalog ordering hint (lower = earlier). Valoon may adjust it.

tagline.description: One sentence shown on catalog cards.

description.description: Detail-page paragraph. Plain text, no markdown.

highlights.description: Bullet list of what it does best.

prerequisites.description: What the user needs before connecting.

key.description: Snake_case key under which the value arrives in the handoff payload's config object.

type.description: Input widget. password values are masked in the UI and never logged.

options.description: select only: the selectable values (sent verbatim in the payload).
